title: hovenia acerba var. acerba
intro: Hovenia acerba var. acerba, also known as Japanese raisin tree or oriental raisin tree, is a plant species native to East Asia, recognized for its small, edible fruits resembling raisins. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to aid in the treatment of hangovers and alleviate symptoms associated with excessive alcohol consumption, as it contains the flavonoid rutin and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Hovenia acerba var. acerba include its potential to reduce liver damage, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Hovenia acerba var. acerba is often used in traditional herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, to support liver function and alleviate hangover symptoms.
